title: Configure IP Token on the network interface ("ip token set")
body:
### Feature request description

## Purpose
An IP Token is useful when a container wants to use SLAAC and privacy addresses but still needs some predictable interface identifier. This is useful for example for services that need direct connection reachability (for example media or http servers) but are in a residential network where the IPv6 prefix regularly changes (for example: in Germany it is common to get a new prefix every 24hours).  Firewalls (like in the popular FRITZ!Box models) allow configuring the port-forwarding/firewall to allow specify an interface-id in that is allowed to reach from the outside.

However, without an IP token (and EUI64 or stable-privacy generated SLAAC addresses), this interface-id is random and therefore not reachable by clients as the firewall of the router will not allow incoming connections there.

### Suggest potential solution

Ability to specify an IP token on the container, for example via a new `token=` `OPTION` when specifying network. For example:

`--network systemd-macvlan:mac=aa:bb:cc:dd,token=::aaaa:cccc:dddd:eeee`

### Have you considered any alternatives?

Right now this is difficult to achieve, current workarounds i use (i run quadlets):

(Enable autoconf via sysctl in `ExecStartPost=`, like: `ExecStartPost=bash -c "nsenter -t $(podman inspect --format '{{.State.Pid}}' systemd-plex) -n sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.eth0.autoconf=1"`) - see https://github.com/containers/netavark/issues/783#issuecomment-3407193971

###  Run `ip set token` via ExecStartPost and `nsenter`
#### Drawbacks
* Does not work with SELinux and --userns=auto out of the box
* Does require the `ip` binary to be available inside the image

### Use sidecar container that joins the network namespace
#### Drawbacks
* Clunky to setup and configure
* IP token gets configured some time after container startup, which can be problematic for some applications to pick it up in time

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body: This would need to be implemented in netavark. I have not checked if the netlink lib supports the required attributes for this already.
